steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Word-in-context vocabulary. Setup: binary-star conditions make planet formation "nearly impossible," so it's "not surprising" that explanation has been lacking. Rafikov and Silsbee finally provided one using a "complex set of factors." The blank describes the kind of explanation that has been missing.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: Because the eventual explanation is complex, what had been missing is a simple/easy one. "Straightforward" (simple, uncomplicated) fits the contrast.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘a discernible’: would mean no detectable explanation existed at all, but the passage implies explanations have been attempted — just not simple ones. Too strong/wrong nuance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘an inconclusive’: saying it "lacked an inconclusive explanation" would mean inconclusive explanations were missing, the opposite of what's meant. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘an unbiased’: bias is never discussed; nothing suggests prior explanations were partial or prejudiced. Out of scope. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
